full_content:
# Drawing Title-Block Metadata Community: REVISION DESCRIPTION, NO., and DATE (A-461)

This community is centered on technical drawing/document title-block metadata fields that standardize how revision information, numbering, and dates are recorded on engineering sheets. The entities include labeled metadata fields—REVISION DESCRIPTION, NO., and DATE—plus language variants of the date field ("날짜") and a year placeholder ("2020"). Relationships show that REVISION DESCRIPTION is associated with a specific drawing sheet (A-461), that revision number and revision description are paired in the drawing metadata, and that date-related labels and values appear together across the title block and specific plan sheets (including a sanitary/landing floor plumbing plan referencing the year/date).

## REVISION DESCRIPTION functions as a revision-history metadata label tied to drawing A-461.

REVISION DESCRIPTION is defined as an engineering-drawing/document metadata field and title-block label used to record revision details, notes, and the history of changes made to a drawing. In this community, it is explicitly linked to the sheet identifier A-461 as a metadata field on that sheet, indicating that revision tracking is operationalized at the document level rather than being purely conceptual. [Data: Entities (107); Relationships (153)].

The grounding text emphasizes that the field appears in the drawing’s title block or document header as an English label for revision-description, serving as the place where revision information is documented for the file/drawing record. This implies the community’s “structure” is largely about standardized documentation fields that help maintain change traceability across technical artifacts. [Data: Entities (107)].

## Revision number and revision description are recorded as paired metadata fields to enumerate revisions consistently.

The community includes a second metadata-label entity, NO., which is described as an English label used in drawing metadata and schedule tables to indicate numbering such as drawing number, sheet/serial number, item number, or revision number. The relationships further indicate that REVISION DESCRIPTION and NO. are paired metadata fields on a drawing sheet, meaning the system treats the revision identifier (number) and its description as coupled information. [Data: Entities (257, 107); Relationships (3584)].

This pairing is operationally important for document control because it supports unambiguous interpretation of “what revision” and “what changed.” The grounding text describes NO. as the label for the numbering field in the title block, used to enumerate revisions or entries, reinforcing that the community is organized around consistent metadata semantics rather than ad hoc free text. [Data: Entities (257)].

## DATE is a standardized English title-block label for drawing/document/revision dates, and it appears alongside other document-control labels.

DATE is defined as the English label for a date field in drawing metadata and the title block, appearing in the title block or revision section to indicate when the drawing or revision was created or updated. In the community’s relationships, DATE is linked to a Korean term ("일련번호") in a set of metadata fields that includes serial number and date labels, which suggests that date control is part of a broader document-control block. [Data: Entities (106); Relationships (613)].

Additionally, the community includes a Korean label variant for the same date field ("날짜") and a relationship that maps "날짜" to DATE, indicating bilingual/multilingual labeling for the same underlying date metadata concept. This reinforces that the community’s impact is primarily in standardization and clarity of document control fields for technical drawings. [Data: Entities (3383); Relationships (4738)].

## The Korean label "날짜" maps to the DATE title-block field, showing multilingual label coverage.

The entity "날짜" is described as a date field shown in the title block of the technical document, establishing that it is not merely a translation artifact but a functional label appearing in documentation. A relationship explicitly connects "날짜" to the DATE entity, stating that Korean and English labels refer to the date field in the title block. [Data: Entities (3383); Relationships (4738)].

This bilingual linkage implies the community supports consistent interpretation of document dates across language contexts. Within this community, multilingual alignment is achieved by mapping the Korean term to the English metadata label, enabling analysts, reviewers, or downstream systems to treat the date field as the same concept regardless of language. [Data: Relationships (4738)].

## The year placeholder "2020" appears as the date value/timeframe used in drawing sets and specific plan sheets.

The entity 2020 is described as the year shown in the drawing date field and on the drawing sheets as the date placeholder in the title block. It indicates the document or plan revision timeframe and serves as the referenced year for the associated drawing set. [Data: Entities (1923)].

Relationships show that a drawing/planning artifact (a sanitary plumbing plan, specifically a landing floor plumbing plan) includes the year/date value. While the relationship wording indicates the year/date appears on those plan sheets, it is grounded through the entity definition that 2020 serves as the year/date placeholder in the title-block date field. [Data: Relationships (2926); Entities (1923)].

## The community is primarily a document-control metadata ecosystem rather than an operational network of actors.

Across the provided entities and relationships, there are no people, organizations, or external systems described; instead, the community consists of standardized labels and values used in engineering drawings’ title blocks (revision description, numbering, and dates). For example, REVISION DESCRIPTION is tied to a specific sheet identifier (A-461), and DATE/"날짜" are tied via bilingual mapping. [Data: Relationships (153, 4738); Entities (107, 106, 3383)].

This structure suggests the “community” is an information model used to enforce consistency in technical documentation. Consequently, the most direct “impact” concerns accuracy, traceability, and interpretability of document control fields (revision enumeration and dates), rather than actions taken by malicious actors. [Data: Entities (107, 257, 106, 1923, 3383)].

## Revision control fields (REVISION DESCRIPTION + NO. + DATE) likely support traceability of changes across related drawing sets.

The relationships indicate that REVISION DESCRIPTION is associated with revision metadata on sheet A-461, that it is paired with NO. to represent revision number plus revision description, and that DATE/"날짜" labels and their values appear in title blocks and plan sheets. Together, these establish a consistent pattern: revisions are identified (NO.), explained (REVISION DESCRIPTION), and time-stamped (DATE/"날짜" and year value such as 2020). [Data: Entities (107, 257, 106, 3383, 1923); Relationships (153, 3584, 4738, 2926)].

From a decision-support perspective, such a system can improve auditability and review workflows because it standardizes where reviewers look for change history. Conversely, any inconsistency in these fields could undermine traceability, leading to confusion about which revision is current and when it was created/updated—an operational risk in engineering documentation processes. [Data: Entities (107, 257, 106); Relationships (3584, 2926)].
